acceptance_targets: [chapter_10_dialogue_001] Pass if the revised exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:309 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:323 contains at least one visible restart or correction from Prue and at least one concrete reference to immediate upstairs harm, and if the narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:25 no longer tells the reader that a sentence 'landed.' Fail if Prue still gets multiple one-line aphoristic finishes in the same confrontation.

[chapter_10_prose_001] Pass if, after revision, no more than two beats in the chapter still use the exact negation-reset structure exemplified at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:85 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:295, and at least two of the cited moments now read through direct image or sensation without first negating an alternative reading. Fail if the cited beats still repeat the same explanatory sentence formula.

[chapter_10_craft_001] Pass if the revised stretch between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:337 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:357 no longer restates all four anchors and Hogwarts's stress pattern in summary form, and if the ending arrives through either a new scene-level consequence or a tighter handoff into Prue's exit. Fail if the chapter still pauses after the argument to recap information the reader already has.

[FA-002] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3222-3280 still leaves the reader clear on the ethical divide but no longer yields multiple standalone mic-drop lines in sequence. Fail if the scene still sounds workshopped rather than overheard under fatigue and pressure.

[FA-007] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3042-3044 still communicates how the table answers physically but does so through a non-teeth sensory channel. Fail if the scene still relies on the same oral-pressure shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:309 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:323 contains at least one visible restart or correction from Prue and at least one concrete reference to immediate upstairs harm, and if the narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:25 no longer tells the reader that a sentence 'landed.' Fail if Prue still gets multiple one-line aphoristic finishes in the same confrontation.
findings.problem: Prue's voice is mostly consistent with the style bible's dry, carefully informal intelligence, but the confrontation keeps resolving into polished mic-drop lines, and the narration explicitly tells the reader one sentence 'landed.' That shifts the scene from pressured speech toward composed thesis exchange, which is exactly the literary-polish failure the dialogue rules warn against.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the confrontation beats centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:25, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:309, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:317, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:323, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:357. Remove the narrator's landing-callout, and roughen Prue's side of the argument so at least two of her turns arrive through interruption, self-repair, repetition, or concrete example rather than clean quotable compression.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after revision, no more than two beats in the chapter still use the exact negation-reset structure exemplified at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:85 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:295, and at least two of the cited moments now read through direct image or sensation without first negating an alternative reading. Fail if the cited beats still repeat the same explanatory sentence formula.
findings.problem: The narration leans too often on the same negation-reset sentence shape: first deny a reading, then supply the correct one. Used this many times in one chapter, the pattern turns from analytic voice into a prose tic and flattens distinct beats into the same explanatory rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the moments anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:85, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:207, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:295. Keep Owen's precise close-third texture, but recast at least two of those beats so they land through image, bodily response, or concrete comparison rather than the exact 'not X / it was Y' correction pattern.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised stretch between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:337 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:357 no longer restates all four anchors and Hogwarts's stress pattern in summary form, and if the ending arrives through either a new scene-level consequence or a tighter handoff into Prue's exit. Fail if the chapter still pauses after the argument to recap information the reader already has.
findings.problem: After the Owen-Prue rupture lands, the chapter cools itself with a recap paragraph that re-summarizes the four-anchor discovery and the Hogwarts stress logic the scene has already earned. That procedural digest weakens the immediate human fallout and blunts the exit's force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the back-third transition anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:337, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:339, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:341. Either dramatize one fresh consequential beat while they finish the work, or compress the routine summary hard and move faster to Prue's altered departure at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:345 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:357 without re-listing the chapter's discoveries in atlas form.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3222-3280 still leaves the reader clear on the ethical divide but no longer yields multiple standalone mic-drop lines in sequence. Fail if the scene still sounds workshopped rather than overheard under fatigue and pressure.
findings.problem: The first major Owen-Prue ethical argument becomes too rhetorically finished. In snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3222-3280, lines such as 'the nearest thing,' 'figures in the margin,' and 'A human one' land as composed thesis statements rather than as the broken, pressure-shaped speech of two exhausted students discovering the argument while speaking it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3222-3280 without softening the disagreement. Add at least a few interruptions, self-corrections, partial restarts, or slight topic-slippage so the exchange feels found under sleepless strain rather than polished for maximum quotability. Keep the underlying moral split exactly as severe; change the spoken texture.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3042-3044 still communicates how the table answers physically but does so through a non-teeth sensory channel. Fail if the scene still relies on the same oral-pressure shorthand.
findings.problem: Recurring somatic-vocabulary pattern: old-work perception keeps returning to teeth/molars/jaw across 7 chapters, and this chapter's 'feel in his teeth' at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3042 repeats the default instead of exploiting the greenhouse cold, lamp strain, or bodily closeness already present in the scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the teeth-based sensation in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3042-3044 with a distinct local cue that belongs to this moment: strain in the wrist, cold through the glass, pulse in the burn, or visual distortion from the overlay work. Avoid mouth/jaw/teeth language altogether in that span.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the premise's sharpest contradiction: school-year comedy, adolescent awkwardness, punitive cruelty, and cosmic dread may coexist in the same novel without canceling each other. When a scene offers a cleaner version and a truer version, choose the truer one unless the character is actively refusing perception on the page.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If pain, cruelty, fear, or moral ugliness would leave a bodily or environmental trace, render that trace instead of summarizing it into tasteful haze.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High. Contractions are the spoken norm for most characters; fuller forms are reserved for Snape, ritual language, or deliberate emphasis.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density, dialogue load, and movement mode aggressively: confrontation against quiet meals, subterranean revelation against corridor farce, ritual compression against spring drift, so no consecutive chapters feel built from the same emotional or structural template.
prose_style_profile.diction: British boarding-school realism under wartime pressure, with speculative vocabulary introduced only when Owen's attention earns it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third limited through Owen, with only brief sensory widenings that he could plausibly notice in the moment.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate analytic accumulation with blunt corporeal sentences. Quiet chapters should breathe; punishment and ritual chapters should tighten without clipping grammar.
